probable_causes: Impeller imbalance due to wear, erosion, or deposit buildup
Misalignment between pump and motor coupling
Cavitation due to insufficient NPSH (Net Positive Suction Head)
diagnostic_procedures: Record vibration readings at all bearing locations (H, V, A)
Check coupling alignment using dial indicator or laser alignment tool
Verify suction pressure and compare with NPSH required
Inspect bearing condition — temperature, grease quality, play
corrective_actions: Replace or repair impeller if worn or imbalanced
Realign pump-motor coupling to within OEM tolerance
Improve suction conditions — raise suction tank level, reduce suction line losses
preventive_actions: Include pump vibration in regular condition monitoring rounds
Schedule alignment check after every motor or pump replacement
Maintain minimum suction head above NPSH required
Periodically check and tighten foundation bolts
safety_precautions: Ensure pump is isolated and de-energized before inspection
Drain pump casing before opening — may contain hot water under pressure
Use hearing protection in pump house areas with high noise levels

probable_causes: Seal face wear from normal aging or abrasive particles
Seal face damage from dry running or thermal shock
diagnostic_procedures: Observe leakage rate — differentiate between drip and spray
Check seal flush water pressure and flow
Measure shaft runout at seal location
Inspect seal faces under magnification for scoring or chipping
corrective_actions: Replace mechanical seal assembly with matching OEM specification
Correct shaft runout if exceeding limits (may require shaft sleeve replacement)
Ensure proper seal setting height during installation
Verify seal flush system is operating correctly
preventive_actions: Maintain clean seal flush water supply
Avoid dry running — ensure pump priming before start
Track seal life and plan replacement before failure
Install seal leak detection sensors where available
safety_precautions: Hot water or chemical leakage is a burn hazard — wear appropriate PPE
Isolate pump from system before seal replacement

probable_causes: Impeller wear ring clearance exceeding limits
Internal recirculation due to worn wear rings
diagnostic_procedures: Plot current operating point on pump performance curve
Verify suction line is free of air pockets — check vent valves
corrective_actions: Replace worn wear rings to restore clearances
Overhaul pump if multiple components are degraded
preventive_actions: Record pump head/flow/power at regular intervals to track degradation
Clean suction strainer during planned shutdowns
Plan wear ring replacement based on clearance trending
safety_precautions: Follow LOTO procedures before pump disassembly
Support heavy components (casing, impeller) with proper lifting devices
